assertedBy: OpenEvo Computational Curriculum Studies Working Group
rationale: OE-INTERDISCIPLINARY's Selection (OE-CONCEPT-000213), biology-discipline sense, and NGSS's HS-LS4-4 ("Construct an explanation based on evidence for how natural selection leads to adaptation of populations", OE-COMPETENCY-000150) describe the same mechanism: differential persistence/reproduction of heritable variants driving population-level change. Not asserted as skos:exactMatch because HS-LS4-4 is a performance expectation (an assessable student action: "construct an explanation") rather than a concept definition — its scope is somewhat narrower (adaptation outcomes specifically) than OE-CONCEPT-000213's full four-discipline definition, of which this is only the biology sense. This is the strongest of the three alignments drafted alongside it (OE-ALIGN-000004, OE-ALIGN-000005) — official science standards and OECB's own biology-sense definition agree closely, unlike the more speculative AI-analogy alignments.
subject: OE-INTERDISCIPLINARY-v1.0.0:OE-CONCEPT-000213
